mission.title: wasmcloud: [FEATURE] `wash up` should have a flag to specify a NATS server config
mission.description: [FEATURE] `wash up` should have a flag to specify a NATS server config. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current wasmcloud deployment
steps.description:
Verify your wasmcloud version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
helm list -n wasmcloud 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working wasmcloud installation.

steps.title: Review wasmcloud configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant wasmcloud configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get configmap -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmcloud
```
### Affected project(s)

- [X] wasmCloud CLI (wash)

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [FEATURE] `wash up` should have a flag to specify a NATS…
steps.description:
## Related Issues
## Release Information
Next

## Consumer Impact

## Testing

### Unit Test(s)

### Acceptance or Integration

### Manual Verification
ran `cargo run -- up --nats-config-file ~/Desktop/Wasm/special.conf --nats-host="127.0.0.1" --nats-port=4959` which executed as expected and also
```yaml
➜ wash up --help | grep nats
      --nats-credsfile <NATS_CREDSFILE>
      --nats-remote-url <NATS_REMOTE_URL>
      --nats-connect-only
      --nats-version <NATS_VERSION>
          NATS server version to download, e.g. `v2.10.7`. See https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server/releases/ for releases [en
v: NATS_VERSION=] [default: v2.10.7]
      --nats-host <NATS_HOST>
      --nats-port <NATS_PORT>
          NATS server port to connect to. This will be used as the NATS listen port if `--nats-connect-only` isn't set [env: WAS
MCLOUD_NATS_PORT=]
      --nats-websocket-port <NATS_WEBSOCKET_PORT>
   
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get events -n wasmcloud --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[FEATURE] `wash up` should have a flag to specify a NATS…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:

### Manual Verification
ran `cargo run -- up --nats-config-file ~/Desktop/Wasm/special.conf --nats-host="127.0.0.1" --nats-port=4959` which executed as expected and also exited normally by `cargo run -- down -- all` stopping all components successfully

verified all
codeSnippets:
➜ wash up --help | grep nats

      --nats-connect-only
      --nats-version <NATS_VERSION>
          NATS server version to download, e.g. `v2.10.7`. See https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server/releases/ for releases [en
v: NATS_VERSION=] [default: v2.10.7]
      --nats-host <NATS_HOST>
      --nats-port <NATS_PORT>
          NATS server port to connect to. This will be used as the NATS listen port if `--nats-connect-only` isn't set [env: WAS
MCLOUD_NATS_PORT=]
      --nats-websocket-port <NATS_WEBSOCKET_PORT>
      --nats-js-domain <NATS_JS_DOMAIN>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with wasmcloud installed or the issue environment reproducible.
